health_conditions: tendon nodule at base of middle finger
received cortisone injection approximately one year prior

benefits.description: Tendon nodule on palm at base of middle finger essentially disappeared after three weeks of use; only smallest vestige remains

protocol_notes: OP explicitly caveats that correlation is not causation and does not claim KLOW was directly responsible. Nodule had persisted for ~1.5 years, was ruled not trigger finger or DMD by a doctor, and cortisone injection ~1 year prior provided only 1-2 months of relief. Surgery was being considered as next step.
